response:
Sec. 1  
[THE WRITER HAS TWO PUBLISHER OFFERS TO CONSIDER BASED ON SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 2  
[PUBLISHER A OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 3000 DOLLARS PLUS 5 PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 3  
[PUBLISHER B OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 1500 DOLLARS PLUS 10 PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 4  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE THAN PUBLISHER B, WE SET UP AN INEQUALITY.]  

Sec. 5  
[THE INEQUALITY IS 3000 PLUS 0.05X GREATER THAN 1500 PLUS 0.10X.]  

Sec. 6  
[SOLVING THE INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X GIVES X LESS THAN 30000.]  

Sec. 7  
[WITHIN THE ANTICIPATED SALES RANGE OF 10000 TO 40000 DOLLARS, PUBLISHER A IS BETTER WHEN X IS BETWEEN 10000 AND 30000.]  

Sec. 8  
[THUS, THE WRITER SHOULD CHOOSE PUBLISHER A IF SALES ARE BETWEEN 10000 AND 30000 DOLLARS.]
